records.ety: [OE. writhen, AS. wrī<?/an to twist; akin to OHG. rīdan, Icel. rī<?/a, Sw. vrida, Dan. vride. Cf. Wreathe, Wrest, Wroth.]
records.morph: [imp. Writhed (?); p. p. Writhed, Obs. or Poetic Writhen (<?/); p. pr. & vb. n. Writhing.]

defs: To twist; to turn; now, usually, to twist or turn so as to distort; to wring.
quotes: Then Satan first knew pain, And writhed him to and fro.
Her mouth she writhed, her forehead taught to frown.
His battle-writhen arms, and mighty hands.

defs: To wrest; to distort; to pervert.
quotes: The reason which he yieldeth showeth the least part of his meaning to be that whereunto his words are writhed.

defs: To extort; to wring; to wrest.
quotes: The nobility hesitated not to follow the example of their sovereign in writhing money from them by every species of oppression.

defs: To twist or contort the body; to be distorted; as, to writhe with agony. Also used figuratively.
quotes: After every attempt, he felt that he had failed, and writhed with shame and vexation.
